title: Local Weyl Description Does Not Fix Global Lattice Consistency
summary: A local Weyl cone only describes the neighborhood of one crossing; it does not by itself guarantee that a globally periodic lattice model exists with the same isolated chirality assignment.
assumptions: One starts from a local continuum Hamiltonian.
regime: Comparing local continuum models with global lattice realizations.
scope: Lecture One transition from local Weyl physics to Brillouin-zone constraints.

source_anchors.notes: The theorem is precisely the obstruction to promoting a lone local chirality into a lattice theory.
Explicit periodic models show multiple nodes rather than a single isolated chirality.

representation: Misuse warning about extrapolating a local cone to a global lattice model.
failure_modes: Ignoring Brillouin-zone compactness leads to false expectations about isolated net chirality.
retrieval_hints: Return this whenever a local Weyl Hamiltonian is treated as a complete lattice model.
